$comment: Agent contract for web_agent. Two logical tools, each with an `operation` discriminator. The CLI surface (`python -m web_agent ...`) is the canonical invocation; this file documents the operation grammar.

tools.name: Inspector
tools.purpose: Capture, query, read, and act on DOM elements via stable handles. Snapshot data lives on disk so it never floods the agent's context — the agent receives a receipt and queries against the snapshot id.

inspect.notes: Run after every navigation or DOM-mutating action.

query.cli: python -m web_agent query <inspect_id> [--role R] [--name TEXT] [--text TEXT] [--tag T] [--scope-selector S] [--all-visibility] [--limit N]
query.returns: {matches: [{handle, role, name, tag, visible, enabled, bbox, value}], shown, total, hint?}
query.notes: Default limit is 20. Truncated results include a hint on how to narrow.

read.returns: {element: {... full record incl. attrs, selector, backend_node_id ...}}

act.errors: stale_handle when the snapshot is out of date — re-run inspect.

tools.name: Browser
tools.purpose: Page-wide escape hatches that don't need a handle.

global_flags.--port: CDP port (default 9222). Start Chrome via `python tools/browser.py start`.

output_contract: Every command writes a single JSON object to stdout. Errors set `ok: false` and exit non-zero, with `kind`, `error`, and `hint` fields the agent should read.
